meanings.definition: go or come back to place, condition, or activity where one has been before
examples: return to your native land
the professor returned to his teaching position after serving as Dean
render money

meanings.definition: go back to a previous state
examples: We reverted to the old rules
This harks back to a previous remark of his

meanings.definition: bring back to the point of departure

examples: return a compliment
return her love
return a kickback
Her old vigor returned
Please refund me my money
render a verdict
deliver a judgment
The estate fell to my sister
The land returned to the family
The estate devolved to an heir that everybody had assumed to be dead

meanings.definition: return to a previous position; in mathematics
examples: The point returned to the interior of the figure
The cow brings in 5 liters of milk
This year's crop yielded 1,000 bushels of corn
The estate renders some revenue for the family

meanings.definition: submit (a report, etc.) to someone in authority
examples: submit a bill to a legislative body

meanings.definition: tending to return to an earlier state
